participants.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official record in that 42 community volunteers with knee OA meeting ACR criteria were recruited.
participants.extracted: Forty-two adults with knee osteoarthritis were enrolled from 50 assessed for eligibility; 6 were excluded for not meeting inclusion criteria and 2 refused participation. Eligible participants had a prediagnosed case of knee OA according to the American College of Rheumatology with radiological evidence of primary osteoarthritis of Kellgren-Lawrence grade 3 or less, were aged 40 to 65 years, and had unilateral or bilateral knee involvement with pain in and around the knee. Participants with knee deformity, hip or back deformity, central or peripheral nervous system involvement, recent steroid use or intra-articular injection within the previous 3 months, inability to cooperate, or physiotherapy in the past 6 months were excluded. The final sample included 21 participants in the experimental group and 21 in the control group, with mean ages of 54.9 and 56.0 years, respectively.
participants.official:
Knee OA recruitment
42 community volunteers
ACR criteria

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description is a home-based 5-week program with a different exercise schedule and only ultrasound as needed, whereas the official intervention was an entirely in-person clinic program with 50 supervised exercise sessions and 6 ultrasound occasions.
interventions.extracted: The experimental group received a 5-week home-based program of isometric therapeutic exercise for knee osteoarthritis, consisting of isometric quadriceps contractions, straight leg raising, and isometric hip adduction. Exercises were performed 5 days per week in sets of 10 repetitions, progressing from 1 set twice daily in week 1 to 2 sets twice daily through week 3 and then 3 sets twice daily through week 5. In addition, both groups received ultrasound therapy (1.5 W/cm2, continuous mode, 7 minutes) applied to the tender point around the knee joint as needed prior to exercise.
interventions.official:
Clinic, supervised individual setting, delivered entirely in‐person
1. Ultrasound: 6 synchronous contact occasions
2. Ultrasound + exercise (strengthening): 50 sessions (10 x per week over 5 weeks); 50 synchronous contact occasions

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes match the official pain and physical function measures and timing, with quadriceps strength as an additional assessed outcome rather than a contradiction.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure pain intensity, knee function, and isometric quadriceps strength at baseline (week 0) and at the end of the intervention at week 5. Pain intensity was assessed using the Numerical Rating Scale (NRS). Knee function/functional disability was assessed using a reduced WOMAC index. Isometric quadriceps strength was assessed using a strength gauge device.
outcomes.official:
At 5 weeks (immediately post‐intervention)
Pain (global/overall pain VAS/NRS)
Physical function (reduced WOMAC function scale)
No quality of life measure
No participant‐reported treatment success measure
